name: Kerry Casserole
recipeInstructions: (Irish pork belly has a fatty layer, which is essential for flavor. Aim to get pork with about equal amounts of meat and fat for the best taste.) Cut the meat into one-inch cubes. Peel and thinly slice the potatoes. Peel or wipe mushrooms and slice if necessary. Grease an ovenproof dish with some butter and place a layer of sliced potatoes at the bottom. Add half the meat pieces, seasoned with rosemary. Layer with half the vegetables, then another layer of potatoes. Repeat using all the remaining meat, vegetables, and potatoes. Pour stock over and dot the potatoes with the remaining butter. Cover and cook in a moderate oven (325F) for 2 hours and 15 minutes. Uncover for the last 30 minutes to brown the potatoes.
